messages.content: Can you explain fine-tuning?
Fine-tuning is like giving an AI extra practice. It already knows many things, but we help it get better at one special job.
Can you explain quantization?
Quantization makes an AI smaller. It is like packing a big toy into a smaller box so it takes up less space.
Can you explain an API?
An API helps two apps talk to each other. It is like a waiter who takes your order to the kitchen and brings back your food.
Can you explain machine learning?
Machine learning is when a computer learns from examples. If you show it many pictures of cats, it can learn what cats usually look like.
Can you explain a neural network?
A neural network is a computer idea that learns in small steps. Each step passes information to the next step until the computer makes an answer.
Can you explain gradient descent?
Gradient descent helps an AI fix its mistakes. It takes tiny steps toward a better answer, like slowly walking downhill.
Can you explain overfitting?
Overfitting is when an AI memorizes examples instead of really learning. It is like memorizing one homework page but not understanding the lesson.
Can you explain LoRA?
LoRA is a way to teach an AI using only a few small new parts. We do not change the whole AI, so training is easier.
Can you explain QLoRA?
QLoRA is like LoRA, but it also makes the AI smaller while training. This helps it fit on computers with less memory.
Can you explain recursion?
Recursion is when something uses itself again and again. In coding, a function can call itself to solve smaller parts of a problem.
Can you explain authentication?
Authentication checks who you are. A password is one way a computer checks that you are really you.
Can you explain authorization?
Authorization checks what you are allowed to do. For example, you may be allowed to read a file but not delete it.
Can you explain cloud computing?
Cloud computing means using someone else's big computers through the internet. It is like borrowing computer power when you need it.
Can you explain binary search?
Binary search is a fast way to find something in a sorted list. You check the middle, then keep only the half where the answer could be.
Can you explain a database index?
A database index helps find things faster. It is like the index at the back of a book that tells you where to look.
Can you explain object-oriented programming?
Object-oriented programming means building code with objects. An object can remember things and do actions, like a toy car having a color and being able to move.
Can you explain a loss function?
A loss function tells an AI how wrong its answer was. A big loss means a big mistake. A small loss means a small mistake.
Can you explain transformers in AI?
A transformer is a kind of AI that is good with words. It looks at many words and decides which ones matter most.
Can you explain attention in AI?
Attention helps an AI focus. It is like when you listen carefully to the most important words in a sentence.
Can you explain embeddings?
Embeddings turn words into numbers. Words with close meanings get numbers that are close together.
Can you explain tokens?
Tokens are tiny pieces of text. An AI reads text in little pieces instead of reading everything all at once.
Can you explain a prompt?
A prompt is what you ask or tell an AI. It is the instruction that helps the AI know what to do.
Can you explain training data?
Training data is the practice material for an AI. The AI looks at many examples and learns from them.
Can you explain inference?
Inference is when an AI uses what it learned to answer a new question. Training is practice. Inference is using the practice.
Can you explain a GPU?
A GPU is a computer part that can do lots of math very fast. AI uses GPUs because training needs a lot of math.
